pattern: Check every length-2 substring of s against the set of length-2 substrings of its reverse.
hints: The reverse of s contains substring 'ba' exactly when s contains substring 'ab'; so a 2-char window (a,b) from s appears in reverse(s) iff (b,a) appears in s.
Build a set of all consecutive 2-char pairs in s, then scan s again checking if the reversed pair is also in the set.
Alternatively: collect all 2-char substrings of reverse(s) into a set, then check each 2-char substring of s for membership.

edge_inputs.description: palindrome string all pairs appear in reverse

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    n = rng.randint(1, size)
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase[:5]) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
